California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
August 15, 1991
E. Jane Pool
c j.ty of Encinatas
city clerk and clerk of the Sanitary Districts
527 Encinatas Boulevard
Encinatas, cA 92024
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our Fi.1e No. A-91- 3 4 5
Dear Ms. PooI:
You have reguested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the PoIitical Reform Act ("Act").r/
Your request is for clarification 
concerning the proposed
amendment to Regulation 18730 which if amended provides that an
individual does not need to be designated in a conflict of
interest code for an agency provided: (1) he or she is already
designated in a code for another agency; (2) the geographical
jurisdiction of the agencies is the same; (3) the disclosure
assigned in the code is the sane as is reguired by section a72oo;
and (4) the filing 
officer would be the sane for both agencies.
OUESTI ON
will the proposed amendment to Regulation 18730 precLude the
need to designate individuals in the conflict of interest codes
for the sanitary, vater and fire protection distrj.cts? 
If so,
should their designation be removed at the annual review of the
codes for these boards?
CONCLUS I ON
The sanitary, v/ater and fire protection districts 
do not meet
the criteria as set out in the proposed amendments to Regulation
18730. As such, the city council members who serve as the board
members for the districts, 
as veII as any other designated
L/ covernment code sections 8looo-91015. Al-I statutory
references are to the Government code unl-ess otherwise indicated.
Cornmission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 18ooo; et seq. AII references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the california code of Regulations.
42tt J Strect, Suitc UOrl . I).C). Ihx tltlT . S.rcr:rmcnr(, C..\ 95ti(t4-OttLl7 . (91()) -lll-5(160


Our File No. A-91-346
Page 2
positions, must renain designated in each di,strict's conflict of
interest code.
FACTS
The city of Encinatas was incorporated in october, 1986. At
that tine, the city acquired two previously existing sanitary
districts, 
a f j.re protection district 
and a water district. 
In
each instance, the boundaries of the distticts 
were not
coterminous with the new city, each lying partly within and partly
outside the city limits. 
The filing officers for the $rater
dj.strict and the fire protection district are not the same.
The nerflbers of the city council serve as the Boards of
Directors for att four of the districts 
and are currently
designated in each of the district's 
conflict of interest codes.
It is your belief that the two sanitary districts 
meet aII
the criteria as set out in the Proposed Regulation 18730(b)
section 3, except for the discrepancy in geographical
jurisdiction.
ANALYSIS
In determining whether the board nenbers for the districts,
who are members of the city councj.l, qualify under the proposed
amendments to Regulation 18730, specif j.c criteria nust be met.
At its August 6. 1991, meeting, the Cornmission amended
Regulation 1873o so that it provides that designated enPloyees wh3
are designated in a conflict of interest code for nore than one
agency, need not be subiect to disclosure obligations under both
codes j-f alL of the following apply:
A) The geographical jurisdiction of one agency is the same
as or is wholly included within the jurisdiction of the other
agency ;
B) The disclosure assigned in the code is the same as that
required under Artj.cle 2 of. chapter 7 of the Political 
Reform Act,
Governnent Code sections 872o0; and
c) The filing officer is the same for both agencies.
It is stated in your letter that the district's 
geographical
jurisdictions are not wholly contained r.rithin the boundaries of
Lhe city of Encinatas. The iurisdiction for the city of Encinatas
is the city. 
The jurisdictions of the various districts 
encompass
areas beyond the city's boundaries. As such, the districts 
do not
meet the criteria in the regulation and designated board members
must continue to file disclosure statements for the district.
A city councj.Imember who sits on the various boards may
expand his or her staternent of economic interest to cover


‐ _   Our File No. A-9■
-346
´ ´
“   _  page 3
reportable interests in both jurisdictions and file copies of this
expanded statement, with an original signature, in both
jurisdictions. 
(Regulation 1873o.)
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact roe at (916) 322-590]-.
' 
SJ,ncerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Acting General counsel
(tut y (.-iJ.at-
